headline: Gray zone exposure stabilizes at 467 grounded airframes and $50M-$2B national liability while Raspberry Pi diversion to Russian Shahed/Geran drones creates precedent risk for defense COTS procurement.

lead_story.title: NASAO Grounding Expands to 25 States, $2B Exposure Ceiling
lead_story.body: Oregon/NASAO Revision 2 white paper (Feb 2026) documents 467 drone airframes grounded or restricted across 23-25 states per state DOT survey, with national replacement exposure estimated between $50M and $2B. The grounding primarily affects gray zone entities—Anzu Robotics, Cogito/SPECTA, and SkyRover—that entered procurement pipelines between 2023-2025 before FCC Covered List designation in Dec 2025. No new flags have escalated since yesterday's brief, indicating the crisis has plateaued but remains unresolved with no clear federal replacement funding mechanism in place.
lead_story.action: Conduct an inventory audit by April 18 of all airframes from Anzu, Cogito/SPECTA, SkyRover, and Autel currently in service or on order; cross-reference against state/federal grounding orders; and generate replacement cost estimates using current Blue UAS Framework pricing to submit budget requests before Q2 planning cycles close.

gray_zone.status: FCC Covered List entity with highest gray zone risk score (0.850) and active retail sales of S1 ($359) and X1 ($599) despite federal prohibition on new authorizations.
gray_zone.development: Forensic analysis confirms SkyRover app connects directly to DJI cloud infrastructure with identical encryption keys, and over-the-air update pipeline touches adversary infrastructure. Shell company filings trace to SZ Knowact Robot Technology Co Ltd. No legitimate supply chain independence exists—this is a pass-through architecture with DJI maintaining cryptographic root key control.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: State/local agencies that procured SkyRover between 2023-2025 face immediate operational security risk from direct DJI cloud connectivity; federal agencies face NDAA Section 848 violation exposure if units remain in inventory after awareness of DJI lineage.
gray_zone.action: Ground all SkyRover units immediately, disconnect from network, and initiate security incident reporting per agency protocol; treat all flight data and mission planning information as potentially compromised; do not attempt firmware updates.

gray_zone.status: Subject of Texas DTPA enforcement action filed Feb 18, 2026 in Collin County with 7 counts; no response filed as of April 4 (45+ days frozen).
gray_zone.development: Teardown confirms PCB layouts align exactly with DJI Mavic 3 Enterprise with matching board part numbers, controller RRC01 is relabeled DJI hardware, and firmware is signed with DJI cryptographic keys giving DJI root key control. Product line discontinued due to adversary component shortage. Texas seeks injunctive relief, civil penalties up to $10K/violation, and corrective disclosure orders.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: Raptor series buyers (primarily state DOT, public safety, and infrastructure inspection programs) hold airframes with no ongoing parts/firmware support and potential injunction-driven grounding if Texas prevails; exposure spans 15-20 states based on 2024 Aloft partnership distribution.
gray_zone.action: Freeze new Anzu purchases immediately; existing operators should prepare transition plans assuming 6-12 month end-of-support timeline; budget replacement units from Blue UAS Framework vendors (Skydio, Teal, Vantage) in Q3 procurement cycles.

gray_zone.status: FCC Covered List entity (Dec 22, 2025) with retroactive revocation authority; no new models can receive FCC authorization.
gray_zone.development: Teardown of Specta Air confirms identical PCBs to DJI Air 3, same DJI P1 chipset, and RF emission reports that differ only by date and logo substitution. Technology licensed from DJI but licensing relationship was never disclosed in marketing or procurement documentation. FCC has exercised retroactive revocation authority on similar cases since Oct 2025 vote.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: Municipal and county programs that adopted Specta Air for mapping/inspection operations face potential retroactive decertification; approximately 8-12 state-level deployments identified in procurement databases.
gray_zone.action: Contact FCC Equipment Authorization System to verify current authorization status of Specta models in your inventory; if revocation occurs, units become illegal to operate and must be grounded within 30 days of notice.

gray_zone.status: Named in Section 1709(a)(1) FY2025 NDAA alongside DJI; added to FCC Covered List Dec 22, 2025; FCC revoked certain pre-ban authorizations in Jan 2026; seeking reconsideration at FCC.
gray_zone.development: Autel is flagged as a gray zone entity with technology licensed from adversary entity marked as 'Proprietary (not DJI derivative)', distinguishing it from Anzu/SPECTA/SkyRover pass-through architectures. However, NDAA statutory designation and FCC Covered List inclusion create identical legal barriers. Recent signal: Florida Sheriff and Tulsa deputies operational deployments (EVO II, Dragonfish) documented in 2021-2026 timeframe now face compliance review.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: Autel has deeper federal/state penetration than other gray zone entities due to earlier market entry (2020-2024); EVO and Dragonfish platforms are in 30+ agency inventories; exposure is primarily regulatory (NDAA/FCC) rather than technical architecture compromise.
gray_zone.action: Monitor Autel's FCC reconsideration petition status (decision expected Q2-Q3 2026); if denied, plan 12-18 month transition as Autel has legitimate supply chain and can potentially support existing units longer than DJI pass-throughs; prioritize replacement of federal-funded units first.

supply_chain.signal: 82% probability of allocation-only transition in Q3 2026; 16 of 25 tracked companion computers (64% concentration) use Jetson Orin NX; 13 boards in Forge DB currently at risk; concentration_risk model confidence 1.00.
supply_chain.window: 5-6 months before defense prioritization locks out commercial procurement channels.
supply_chain.action: Place 12-18 month forward orders for Jetson Orin NX modules now while commercial availability remains; negotiate allocation agreements with distributors by May 2026; evaluate QRB5165 successor (Qualcomm) as architectural hedge for programs in design phase.

supply_chain.signal: 87% probability of supply tightening in H2 2026; 15 of 52 tracked thermal cameras (29% concentration) use Boson 640; AeroVironment Switchblade production ramp identified as primary demand driver; cross_correlation model confidence 0.87.
supply_chain.window: 6-9 months before allocation or extended lead times impact new builds.
supply_chain.action: Audit current Boson 640 inventory and pipeline commitments; for Q4 2026 and 2027 deliveries, secure supplier commitments in writing by June 2026 or design in Boson 320 as lower-cost/lower-performance fallback; coordinate with Teledyne FLIR sales engineering on defense priority customers.

supply_chain.signal: 40,000+ Raspberry Pi 4B units confirmed diverted to Russian Geran-2/3 (Shahed-136/238) as 'Borscht Tracker V3'; RPi 5 confirmed in Molniya-2R reconnaissance drone; RPi-based tracker with mesh networking in Geran-5 jet drone; GUR (Ukrainian military intelligence) teardowns provide primary source documentation.
supply_chain.window: Diversion is ongoing; reputational and policy risk is immediate for defense programs using commercial RPi boards.
supply_chain.action: Conduct immediate supply chain provenance review for any programs using Raspberry Pi boards; implement end-use certification and distributor vetting; consider migration to defense-qualified compute modules (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson, NXP i.MX) for new designs to avoid future Congressional scrutiny of COTS component diversion pathways.

watch_list.why: 79% probability that three simultaneous deadlines ground uncertified gray zone drones; 4 regulatory/compliance flags matched with cross_correlation confidence 0.89.
watch_list.trigger: FCC publishes final firmware certification requirements in Q3 2026 or announces enforcement discretion rollback for pre-ban authorizations.

watch_list.why: 81% probability of supply bottleneck in Q3-Q4 2026; only 29 NDAA-compliant options exist from 29 total tracked mesh radios (59% concentration in at-risk vendors); concentration_risk model confidence 1.00.
watch_list.trigger: Lead times for Persistent Systems MPU5, Rajant, or Silvus radios extend beyond 16 weeks or a major defense program issues long-term agreement locking up supply.

watch_list.why: 80% probability QRB5165 successor enters Blue UAS Framework in 2027; 5 of 25 companion computers (20%) currently depend on QRB5165 with no clear migration path announced.
watch_list.trigger: Qualcomm announces QRB5165 end-of-life or ModalAI/other integrators announce next-generation flight controller boards requiring architecture redesign.

predictions.event: FLIR Boson 640 tightens — 3 Boson units tracked, AeroV Switchblade ramp drives allocation-only access for defense customers
predictions.hedge: Place non-cancellable purchase orders for 12-month Boson 640 requirements by June 2026; negotiate Teledyne FLIR defense priority customer status; design Boson 320 compatibility into airframe thermal mounts as fallback.

predictions.event: Jetson Orin NX goes allocation-only for defense (13 boards in Forge DB at risk)
predictions.hedge: Execute 18-month forward buy of Jetson Orin NX modules by May 2026 at current commercial pricing; establish allocation agreements with Arrow, Avnet, or direct NVIDIA defense channel; evaluate Qualcomm QRB5165 as second-source for new programs.

predictions.event: FCC firmware cliff — 3 simultaneous deadlines ground uncertified gray zone drones
predictions.hedge: Accelerate gray zone replacement procurement into Q4 2026 budget cycles to avoid Q1 2027 scramble; pre-qualify Blue UAS Framework vendors (Skydio X10, Teal 2, Vantage Vesper) now; negotiate priority delivery slots with framework vendors for Q4 2026-Q1 2027 window.

signal_summary.top_concern: 467 airframes grounded with no federal replacement funding and Raspberry Pi diversion creating COTS procurement precedent risk
